sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the business problem or strategic value clearly and avoid vague management jargon.
Trust signals should appear early and use honest proof such as case themes, senior roles or industry experience.
Selected cases preview should come before long company story text so visitors see evidence before narrative.
Contact CTA should invite a first conversation or quote request without pressure or exaggerated claims.
Services must be framed around client needs and outcomes, not only internal consulting labels.
Engagement models should explain how work can be structured without implying fixed pricing if none exists.
FAQ should answer practical buying questions briefly and concretely.
Each case card should identify assignment type, client context and outcome in a sober tone.
If exact metrics are unavailable, results-highlights may describe qualitative outcomes but must not invent numbers.
Contact CTA should follow the proof section, inviting similar discussions or requests.
About story should explain focus, background and how the firm works with clients, not generic vision filler.
Team block should emphasize seniority, roles and relevant expertise because buyers often assess who will do the work.
Process steps should use real consulting stages the business actually delivers.
Phone must be a tel: link and email must be a mailto: link.
Contact form should ask only for information needed to start a relevant dialogue.
Any address shown should be readable as plain text even if a map is omitted.
